11_immunization.q: DTaP contains protection against how many organisms?
11_immunization.l: Diphtheria, Tetanus, and Pertussis.

11_immunization.q: Hib vaccine is an example of a:
11_immunization.l: Hib links polysaccharide to protein.

11_immunization.q: PCV13 is a:
11_immunization.l: Pneumococcal conjugate vaccine.

11_immunization.q: PPSV23 is primarily associated with:
11_immunization.l: It is an unconjugated polysaccharide vaccine.

11_immunization.q: Why are polysaccharide vaccines conjugated to proteins?
11_immunization.l: Conjugation makes the response T-cell dependent.

11_immunization.q: A pure polysaccharide antigen usually produces:
11_immunization.l: T-independent responses mainly generate IgM.

11_immunization.q: Which vaccine is unconjugated?
11_immunization.l: PPSV23 lacks a protein carrier.

11_immunization.q: Which vaccine covers Neisseria meningitidis?
11_immunization.l: MCV4 is the meningococcal conjugate vaccine.

11_immunization.q: Viral vector vaccines use:
11_immunization.l: Modified harmless viruses deliver genetic instructions.

11_immunization.q: Which COVID-19 vaccine platform uses a viral vector?
11_immunization.l: Janssen uses an adenoviral vector.

11_immunization.q: mRNA vaccines work by:
11_immunization.l: Host cells produce the antigen from mRNA.

11_immunization.q: Which COVID-19 vaccines are mRNA vaccines?
11_immunization.l: Comirnaty and Spikevax are mRNA vaccines.

11_immunization.q: Which vaccine provides the best example of herd immunity?
11_immunization.l: Sabin vaccine induces intestinal IgA and reduces transmission.

11_immunization.q: Herd immunity protects which group of people?
11_immunization.l: Herd immunity decreases transmission and protects susceptible individuals.

11_immunization.q: Cross-reactivity occurs when antibodies bind to:
11_immunization.l: Cross-reactivity occurs when antibodies recognize similar antigens.

11_immunization.q: Antitoxin antibodies primarily function by:
11_immunization.l: Antitoxin antibodies bind and neutralize toxins.

11_immunization.q: Which vaccine type induces the strongest immune response?
11_immunization.l: Live attenuated vaccines mimic natural infection.

11_immunization.q: Which vaccine type is contraindicated in pregnancy?
11_immunization.l: Live attenuated vaccines should not be given during pregnancy.

11_immunization.q: Which vaccine type is contraindicated in immunocompromised patients?
11_immunization.l: Live attenuated vaccines may cause disease in immunocompromised hosts.

11_immunization.q: Which vaccine is associated with reversion to virulence?
11_immunization.l: Sabin vaccine may rarely revert to virulence.

11_immunization.q: Which vaccine induces both humoral and cellular immunity?
11_immunization.l: Live attenuated vaccines activate both arms of adaptive immunity.

11_immunization.q: Which vaccine mainly induces humoral immunity?
11_immunization.l: Killed vaccines primarily stimulate antibody production.

11_immunization.q: What is the protein carrier in conjugate vaccines used for?
11_immunization.l: Protein carriers convert a T-independent response into a T-dependent response.

11_immunization.q: Conjugate vaccines promote which process?
11_immunization.l: Conjugate vaccines allow class switching and memory formation.

11_immunization.q: Which vaccine induces a predominantly IgM response?
11_immunization.l: PPSV23 is an unconjugated polysaccharide vaccine producing mainly IgM.

11_immunization.q: Which vaccine generates strong memory cell formation?
11_immunization.l: Conjugate vaccines stimulate T-cell dependent immunity and memory.

11_immunization.q: Which vaccine contains 13 pneumococcal serotypes?
11_immunization.l: PCV13 contains 13 pneumococcal capsular serotypes.

11_immunization.q: Which vaccine contains 23 pneumococcal serotypes?
11_immunization.l: PPSV23 contains 23 pneumococcal serotypes.

11_immunization.q: Which vaccine protects against Haemophilus influenzae type b?
11_immunization.l: Hib vaccine protects against encapsulated H. influenzae type b.

11_immunization.q: Which vaccine protects against Neisseria meningitidis serogroups A, C, Y, and W-135?
11_immunization.l: MCV4 is the meningococcal conjugate vaccine.

11_immunization.q: Which component of DTaP is acellular?
11_immunization.l: The pertussis component is acellular.

11_immunization.q: What does the 'T' in DTaP stand for?
11_immunization.l: T = Tetanus toxoid.

11_immunization.q: Which vaccine is an example of a toxoid vaccine?
11_immunization.l: DTaP contains diphtheria and tetanus toxoids.

11_immunization.q: Which vaccine is given orally?
11_immunization.l: Sabin is the oral polio vaccine.

11_immunization.q: Which vaccine is administered by injection and produces serum IgG?
11_immunization.l: Salk is the injectable inactivated polio vaccine.

11_immunization.q: Which vaccine may be neutralized by maternal antibodies?
11_immunization.l: Maternal antibodies can interfere with live attenuated vaccines.

11_immunization.q: What is the main purpose of post-exposure prophylaxis?
11_immunization.l: PEP combines immediate protection with future immunity.

11_immunization.q: Which component of rabies PEP provides immediate protection?
11_immunization.l: Rabies immunoglobulin provides immediate antibodies.

11_immunization.q: Which component of HBV PEP provides immediate protection?
11_immunization.l: HBIG supplies immediate antibodies against HBV.

11_immunization.q: Which component of tetanus PEP provides long-term immunity?
11_immunization.l: The toxoid stimulates active immunity and memory formation.

11_immunization.q: Which vaccine platform delivers mRNA encoding an antigen?
11_immunization.l: mRNA vaccines deliver instructions for antigen synthesis.

11_immunization.q: Which vaccine platform uses a harmless virus to deliver genetic material?
11_immunization.l: Viral vector vaccines use modified viruses as delivery vehicles.
